faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1:
• Application deadline: October 1, 2025
• Tentative response date: October 7, 2025
• Event date: October 17-19, 2025
faq.q2: How does the application process work?
faq.a2: AlegrIA is an exclusive event with limited spots. You must apply with your team and go through a selection process. Only the most promising teams will be accepted to participate in the hackathon. We will notify you if your application was successful.
faq.a4: It's preferable, but not required. We value curiosity and enthusiasm more than technical knowledge.
faq.q5: What do I need to bring?
faq.a5: A laptop, your ideas, and your team. We provide Wi-Fi and power (we can provide Chromebooks if necessary).
faq.a6: It's free to apply. If your team is accepted, there's a registration fee of $20 per person.
faq.q7: Can I go without a team?
faq.a7: No. You must apply with a team of at least 2 people.
faq.a8: Vibe-coding is a style of rapid product creation where participants build functional apps, websites, or tools relying on AI and no-code/low-code platforms. The goal is to ship something functional fast, prioritizing usability and originality over perfect code.
faq.a8_short: Rapid product creation using AI and no-code/low-code platforms. Prioritizes usability and originality over perfect code.
footer.description: Empowering Venezuelans to build the business of their dreams.
speakers.title: Our Speakers
speakers.victor.title: Founder and CEO of Slash
speakers.victor.bio: CEO and co-founder of Slash, a vertical business banking fintech valued at $370 million
speakers.victor.full_bio: Víctor Cárdenas Codriansky, 23 years old, is the CEO and co-founder of Slash, a vertical business banking fintech valued at $370 million after raising $60 million in venture capital. Deeply connected to his Venezuelan identity, he overcame a crisis that nearly led to bankruptcy after the collapse of Yeezy in 2022, demonstrating admirable resilience. During the pandemic, he dropped out of Computer Science studies at Stanford to dedicate himself entirely to the project alongside his best friend, Kevin Bai. Slash, a Y Combinator graduate, integrates accounting software and automates financial processes for SMEs in the US. The company plans to expand to over 100 countries and include Venezuela in its operations by 2026.
speakers.victor.achievements.0: Finalist of the Breakthrough Junior Challenge 2016, ranking among the top 10 out of over 15,000 global projects
speakers.victor.achievements.1: Purchased the slash.com domain for $1 million to strengthen the brand and platform legitimacy
speakers.victor.achievements.2: Y Combinator Summer 2021 graduate, receiving investment and mentorship to scale the business
speakers.victor.achievements.3: Overcame a revenue drop from $5M to $1.5M after the Yeezy crisis, repositioning the company with new products
speakers.victor.achievements.4: Upcoming launch of the Global USD Account in 113 countries, including Venezuela in 2026
speakers.vicente.title: Founder and CEO of Yummy
speakers.vicente.bio: Founder and CEO of Yummy, the leading superapp for delivery and ridesharing in Venezuela
speakers.vicente.full_bio: Vicente Zavarce is the Founder and CEO of Yummy, an e-commerce super-app launched in Venezuela. Valued at over $200 million, Yummy operates in four countries, serves 4 million users, and completes around 800,000 orders each month. A Magna Cum Laude graduate from Northeastern University, Vicente honed his growth marketing skills at Wayfair, Getaround, and Postmates before launching Yummy in 2020 as Venezuela's first delivery app. What started as a food delivery platform has rapidly expanded into groceries, rideshare, and soon digital payments, making Yummy one of Latin America's rising super-apps. Vicente is also a Forbes 30 Under 30 honoree and Partner at Epakon Capital, where he invests in software companies across industries.
speakers.vicente.achievements.0: Fluent in Spanish, English, Portuguese, and French
speakers.vicente.achievements.1: First financed Yummy using personal savings and credit cards before raising venture funding
speakers.vicente.achievements.2: Operated the company remotely from San Francisco during COVID-19 lockdowns
speakers.vicente.achievements.3: Known for his data-driven, resilience-focused leadership style
speakers.pedro.title: Founder and CEO of Cashea
speakers.pedro.bio: Founder and CEO of Cashea, Venezuela's leading buy now, pay later app
speakers.pedro.full_bio: Pedro Julio Vallenilla is the Founder and CEO of Cashea, Venezuela's leading buy now, pay later app. Since its launch in 2022, Cashea has reached over 8 million users and is present in 18,000 stores, becoming one of the most influential financial apps in the country. His innovative Buy Now, Pay Later model transformed how Venezuelans shop, providing payment flexibility to millions of users. With over 31 million orders processed, Cashea has revolutionized Venezuelan e-commerce. Before Cashea, Pedro founded startups like Elemétrica, Meser, and TuDescuento, and worked at Colektia, where he managed million-dollar portfolios for banks like Nubank and Santander. He has also been a mentor in programs like Startup Chile and Founder Institute, supporting entrepreneurs throughout Latin America.
speakers.pedro.achievements.0: Cashea processes around 1% of Venezuelan GDP in transactions
speakers.pedro.achievements.1: The app grew 10× in one year and already covers almost a third of the country's adult population
speakers.pedro.achievements.2: Started in a single shopping mall in Caracas before expanding massively
speakers.pedro.achievements.3: Mentor in programs like Startup Chile and Founder Institute
speakers.pedro.achievements.4: Previous experience at Colektia managing million-dollar portfolios for Nubank and Santander
speakers.pedro_urdaneta.title: President of Venecápital
speakers.pedro_urdaneta.bio: Corporate lawyer with over 25 years of experience in mergers and acquisitions, corporate finance, and cross-border transactions in Venezuela.
speakers.pedro_urdaneta.full_bio: Pedro Urdaneta is a corporate lawyer with over 25 years of experience in mergers and acquisitions, corporate finance, and cross-border transactions in Venezuela. In addition to his role as President of Venecápital —the Venezuelan Private Capital Association— where he drives the development of the private capital industry in the country, he is a Senior Partner at LEĜA Abogados, where he co-leads the Corporate/M&A and Banking & Finance practices. He has been a university professor in subjects such as comparative law and business ethics at Universidad Metropolitana, and is a recurring speaker at international business forums.
speakers.pedro_urdaneta.achievements.0: Recently appointed President of Venecápital for its new statutory period
speakers.pedro_urdaneta.achievements.1: Recognized lawyer by Chambers Global for his expertise in corporate restructuring and infrastructure projects
speakers.pedro_urdaneta.achievements.2: Admitted to practice in both Venezuela and New York, giving him an international perspective on transactions and litigation

ideas.title: Project Ideas
ideas.description: Don't know where to start? Here are some inspiring ideas you could develop during the hackathon.
ideas.idea1.title: Collaborative Traffic Map
ideas.idea1.description: An app or bot that allows drivers to instantly report potholes, police checkpoints, or street closures and see those alerts on a collaborative map.
ideas.idea2.title: Community Price Comparator
ideas.idea2.description: A service where users send prices of basic products in their neighborhood and get an updated average to compare nearby stores.
ideas.idea3.title: Group Contribution Platform
ideas.idea3.description: A simple platform for a group of friends to organize money collections, keep automatic records of who has contributed, and receive reminders before each contribution.
ideas.idea4.title: Anti-Waste Marketplace
ideas.idea4.description: A minimalist marketplace where bakeries or restaurants publish their surplus food at closing time and volunteers can reserve it to avoid waste.
ideas.idea5.title: Collaborative Event Agenda
ideas.idea5.description: A platform where organizers can create attractive pages for their activities and attendees can register, receive reminders, and access all information through a single shared link.
ideas.idea6.title: Citizen Reporting Portal
ideas.idea6.description: A citizen portal where any neighbor uploads photos and location of broken streetlights, leaks, or cracks, with automatic notifications to authorities or community groups.
ideas.idea7.title: Neighborhood Alert Network
ideas.idea7.description: A geolocated bot or service where neighbors publish alerts about robberies, emergencies, or suspicious activity and everyone receives instant notification in a common chat.
ideas.idea8.title: Extracurricular Activities Directory
ideas.idea8.description: A web directory or bot where organizers of workshops and extracurricular activities publish schedules, prices, and descriptions, and parents can filter and enroll their children quickly.
ideas.inspired_cta: Feeling inspired? Apply now and develop your idea!
overview.title: Your chance to stand out
overview.description: A general overview of what you'll experience at AlegrIA.
overview.what_is.title: What is AlegrIA?
overview.what_is.body:
A 48‑hour event where teams create startups benefiting Venezuela from scratch using artificial intelligence tools.
Use no‑code/low‑code tools to build functional prototypes.
overview.flow.title: Agenda
overview.flow.body:
Day 1: Workshop + talks to spark ideas.
Days 2–3: 48h of guided building with mentors.
Finale: Live demos and awards.
overview.eligibility.title: Eligibility
overview.eligibility.body:
Teams of 2–4 people.
Anyone aged 14 to 29 in Venezuela.
Advanced technical experience optional.
overview.dates.title: Key dates
overview.dates.body:
Applications close: October 1, 2025.
Day 1: October 17, 2025.
Hackathon: October 18–19, 2025.
overview.cost.title: Cost
overview.cost.body:
Applying is free.
If accepted: $20 per person.
overview.prizes.title: Prizes
overview.prizes.body:
Over $75,000 in prizes.
Extra software and gear perks.
home.hero.title: Venezuela's first vibe-coding hackathon
home.hero.subtitle: 26 teams. 48 hours. $75,000+ in prizes. October 2025, Caracas.
home.about.headline: In October 2025, 26 teams of young Venezuelans locked in for 48 hours to build startups with artificial intelligence.
home.about.how_title: You bring the idea, AI writes the code
home.about.p1: Vibe-coding is building software by describing what you want in plain language. The AI generates the code. That simple.
home.about.p2: Teams of young people aged 14 to 29 compete with real mentors, real investors and over $75,000 in prizes to turn their ideas into functional products.
home.gallery.title: AlegrIA in schools
home.testimonials.title: What those who lived it say
home.next_edition.title: AlegrIA is back in 2026.
home.next_edition.subtitle: The next edition is coming in full force. Want to be part of it?
hackaton.hero.subtitle: What it is, how it works, who can participate, and what to expect from the most intense weekend of the year.
hackaton.what.title: Venezuela's first vibe-coding hackathon
hackaton.what.p1: AlegrIA is a 48-hour competition where teams of students aged 14 to 29 use artificial intelligence tools to build real startups — no coding experience required.
hackaton.what.p2: Vibe-coding is the practice of building software by describing what you want in plain language, without writing code. Tools like Lovable and Cursor make it possible for anyone with a good idea to build a real product.
hackaton.what.p3: It's a real entrepreneurship experience, with real mentors, real investors, and $75,000+ in real prizes.
hackaton.how.title: Step by step
hackaton.steps.0.title: Apply with your team
hackaton.steps.0.description: Form a team of 2 to 4 people. You don't need coding experience — just curiosity and the drive to build something new.
hackaton.steps.1.title: Conference day
hackaton.steps.1.description: Venezuelan entrepreneurs and investors share their experience. Learn, get inspired, and sharpen your idea before the clock starts.
hackaton.steps.2.title: 48 hours of building
hackaton.steps.2.description: The hackathon begins. You have vibe-coding tools, available mentors, and teammates. The rest is up to you.
hackaton.steps.3.title: Live demo and awards
hackaton.steps.3.description: You present your product in 5 minutes to a panel of entrepreneurs and investors. The best win. Everyone learns.
hackaton.who.title: For young people with vision
hackaton.venue.title: Event venue
hackaton.community.title: AlegrIA in schools
hackaton.community.body: Before the main hackathon, AlegrIA brings vibe-coding workshops to schools in Caracas. More than 8 institutions have already experienced it.
hackaton.organizers.title: The organizers
hackaton.faq.title: Frequently asked questions
hackaton.faq.a1: Venezuelan students between 14 and 29 years old. It doesn't matter if you're in high school or university, or if you've never written a line of code before.
hackaton.faq.q2: Do I need to know how to code?
hackaton.faq.a2: Not at all. We use vibe-coding tools like Lovable and Cursor so anyone can build a working app without traditional code. What you need is an idea and the drive to execute it.
hackaton.faq.a3: Teams are 2 to 4 people. The application process is done as a team, so you need to have your group together before applying. If you don't have a team yet, reach out to people in your school or close circle.
hackaton.faq.a4: Participation is completely free. Just bring your laptop, charger, and the desire to build something new.
hackaton.faq.q5: What should I bring on the day?
hackaton.faq.a5: Your laptop, charger, and lots of energy. Food, drinks, and snacks will be available throughout the weekend.
hackaton.faq.q6: What types of projects can be submitted?
hackaton.faq.a6: Any digital app, tool, or platform that solves a real problem in Venezuela. A web app, a bot, a dashboard, or any other working solution.

ideas.note.p2: The best idea is the one you're passionate about. If you already have something in mind, bring it. If not, use these as a starting point.
